items.summary: Really really good gameplay, pretty good art with some unique designs. Wish all the content was human translated and that the game explained its basic mechanics better.

items.title: [正面] Лучше иметь 37 возможных отцов или играть в геншин...
items.summary: Лучше иметь 37 возможных отцов или играть в геншин, что в принципе тождественно, чем вкатываться в это.В игре регулярно обновляется донатный магазин, но контента нет за 2 года с релиза игры от слова совсем.Баги могут не фикситься неделями а в игре может отвалиться коннект просто потому что.Даже разработчикам ♥♥♥♥♥ на игру и ее совершенно не рекламируют, отсюда 150к долларов нетворс на гача ревенью.Если у вас патологическое желание обмазываться любым говном лучше уж реверс скачайте.В общем hidden gem, рекомендую к ознакомлению

items.title: [正面] Genuinely the best implementation of PvP in a Gach...
items.summary:
Genuinely the best implementation of PvP in a Gacha Game I've ever seen, I really love the PvP in this game. The PvE and story is also wonderfully challenging, and character building is fun and thought provoking. I really like the way that you can beat way harder levels than you should by sheer power of will and cunning. 

Genuinely 10/10

The characters are interesting, the story is horrifying and tragic, and the art is GORGEOUS. I CANNOT recommend this game enough <3333

items.title: [正面] So: is this a good roguelike card game? Yes. Is th...
items.summary:
So: is this a good roguelike card game? Yes. Is this an "expensive" gacha game? No. Is there an engaging plot here? Yes. Is there good English translation? Not quite.

And now in more detail:

The game is excellent, with many interesting mechanics. New ones are added regularly, both character-specific and designed for entire character combinations.
The music is incredibly beautiful, with plenty of it—good and varied. Stylistically, the game is pleasing: thoughtful character designs, good UI/UX, lots of gorgeous CG, and lore for almost every stick.
PvP here doesn't require you to spend any money, and even with non-meta characters you can effectively carry out dirty schemes against other players (30+ wins in a row is saying something).
The gacha system itself is inexpensive; you can play without investing a dollar. There are characters who are "stronger" than others, but everything is decided by straight hands, smart brains, clever tricks - the game provides all sorts of resources for this.

And, compared to other strategy games, the cost of your mistake is almost zero. It's really nice that you don't get any penalties or penalties for "retreating." Didn't work out? Retreat, assemble a new team, and fight again. There are also "resurrections," which are given out freely once a day—they can be used in most content not designed for late-game challenges.

The developers, as far as I can tell, listen to players and make necessary changes literally on the fly, while trying to keep all the content up to date through character balance.

Is the game worth supporting? It's up to everyone to decide, but I've answered that question for myself.
